chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْمُحْرِمِ يُقَبِّلُ امْرَأَتَهُ وَ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهَا بِشَهْوَةٍ أَوْ غَيْرِ شَهْوَةٍ أَوْ يَنْظُرُ إِلَى غَيْرِهَا
chapter_title.en: Chapter 104 – The one in Ihraam kisses his wife and he looks at her with desire, or without desire, or he looks at someone else

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مختلف فيه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/356)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مختلف فيه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (17/356)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنِ الْمُحْرِمِ يُقَبِّلُ أُمَّهُ قَالَ لَا بَأْسَ هَذِهِ قُبْلَةُ رَحْمَةٍ إِنَّمَا يُكْرَهُ قُبْلَةُ الشَّهْوَةِ .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Muhammad Bin Ahmad Al Nahdy, from Muhammad Bin Al Waleed, from Aban Bin Usman, from Al Husayn Bin Hammad who said,
‘I asked Abu Abdullahasws about the one in Ihraam kissing his mother. Heasws said: ‘There is no problem with these kisses of mercy. But what is disliked is kisses of the lustful desires’.
en.sarwar: 9. Ahmad ibn Muhammad from has narrated from Muhammad ibn Ahmad al-Nahdiy from Muhammad ibn al-Walid from Aban ibn ‘Uthman from al-Husayn ibn Hammad who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, about a man who is in the state of Ihram and his mother kisses him. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is not harmful; it is a kiss of blessing, only kissing with lust is despised.’”
